title: Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, buttery tagine dish from Saudi Arabia, featuring tender lamb and a spicy kick.
instructions: Heat butter in the bottom of a traditional tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the cumin and coriander and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chilli flakes, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the dried dates, parsley, and cilantro, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the tagine and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the tagine from the heat and let cool slightly.
Strain the cooking liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and discard the solids.
Return the tagine to the heat and add 2 tablespoons of butter, stirring until melted and smooth.
Serve the lamb hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Let the tagine cool to room temperature, then refrigerate or freeze for up to 3 days.
When ready to serve, remove the tagine from the refrigerator and let sit at room temperature for 30 minutes, then reheat in the oven until hot and bubbly.
